title: SessionId::from_raw accepts any string bypassing the UUID invariant of the newtype
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
kiss
## Location
src/state/categories.rs:42-46
## Violation
The SessionId newtype claims to be UUID-shaped:

```rust
/// Session identifier — UUID-shaped string.
///
/// Newtype so session ids don't get confused with teammate / agent / transcript
/// ids at call sites...
#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]
#[serde(transparent)]
pub struct SessionId(String);
```

But `from_raw` accepts any String:

```rust
/// Wrap a caller-supplied string without re-validating. Used by
/// persist.rs when deserializing v1 files written before this
/// newtype existed.
#[must_use]
pub fn from_raw(s: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
    Self(s.into())
}
```

The test `session_id_round_trips_from_raw` explicitly stores `"abcd-1234"` — not a UUID. The "make invalid states unrepresentable" guideline is violated: SessionId promises UUID, but `from_raw("")` or `from_raw("../../etc/passwd")` produce SessionId values that propagate through logging, paths (session_project_dir uses session_id), and database queries.

Worse, since `#[serde(transparent)]` is on the type, a malicious or corrupted on-disk session file with `"session_id": "../../etc/passwd"` is silently accepted by from_raw — and `session_project_dir.join(session_id.as_str())` is a path traversal vector if any caller composes paths this way.

## Mandated refactor
1. Rename `from_raw` to `from_legacy_v1_blob` and gate it behind `pub(crate)` plus a clarifying doc comment that it is only safe to call on input that was previously a uuid::Uuid::to_string() output.
2. Add a `try_parse(s: &str) -> Result<Self, uuid::Error>` for the deserialization path that validates the input is a valid UUID string.
3. The serde derive should use try_parse via a custom impl rather than transparent; transparent allows any string through.

## Filed from QA review dispatch
